idea: Adapted proven 64x128 1p1c wide-N starting kernel to the 2048x16384x2048 target; verified correctness and established the first custom baseline at 255.305 TFLOPS.

Tried a structural ws1p2c 128x128 tile to raise WGMMA issue on the compute-bound baseline, but the added shared-memory footprint and larger CTA reduced net performance to 221.098 TFLOPS, below iter001.
Increased WARP_N from 128 to 160 on the 1p1c base to raise WGMMA work per producer load, but the wider tile reduced net throughput to 175.719 TFLOPS and underperformed the original iter001 baseline.

Tried the stmatrix compiler flag on the 1p1c fp32-accumulator base after SASS comparison, but Choreo codegen fails because stmatrix store currently requires a 16-bit accumulator type and cannot materialize the fp32 output path.
Persistent 1p1c scheduling preserved the 64x128 microkernel but cut throughput to 115.8 TFLOPS, so keeping CTAs resident per SM did not improve this low-occupancy compute-bound shape.

Adding __launch_bounds__(256, 2) to the proven 1p1c CUDA raised throughput to 261.055 TFLOPS, so explicitly steering Hopper toward two resident CTAs per SM helped this low-occupancy compute-bound kernel.
